description_long: This is a roughly hewn, irregularly shaped stone with a predominantly off-white to light gray coloration, accented by patches of ochre and brown mineral staining. The surface texture is coarse and uneven, with visible striations and natural fractures running across multiple faces, suggesting a sedimentary or metamorphic origin. One face appears relatively flat, while the others are more jagged and angular, giving the stone a blocky yet organic appearance. The stone has a solid, dense look, with no visible cavities or significant porosity, and a small label is affixed to one of its upper surfaces. The coloration and weathering patterns indicate exposure to the elements, with some areas showing more pronounced discoloration and roughness. This type of stone could serve as a natural barrier or obstacle in an urban park, garden, or roadside setting.
description: A blocky, irregular stone with off-white and ochre hues, coarse texture, and visible mineral striations. Its weathered, angular surfaces suggest natural origin and durability.
description_view_0: This view highlights a broad, striated face with subtle ochre staining and a slightly sloped top edge.
description_view_1: This angle shows a taller, more vertical face with pronounced brown and yellow mineral patches and a visible label on the upper surface.
description_view_2: Here, the stone's angularity is evident, with a mix of flat and jagged surfaces and a concentration of ochre coloration near the top.
description_view_3: This side reveals the stone's longest dimension, with a relatively flat, horizontally striated surface and a blend of white and brown tones.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or obstacle on the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular natural striations and layering, with mottled mineral staining in ochre and brown; no artificial carvings or regular patterns.
attribute_weathering: Rough and weathered, with visible mineral deposits and some surface discoloration indicating moderate exposure to the elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with manicured or paved urban environments but can blend with natural landscaping or other stones; may be partially surrounded by grass or soil.
